projects.name: Generations Toronto
projects.blurb: The Government of Canada marked the official opening of Generations Toronto, a rental housing development supported through CMHC's Apartment Construction Loan Program.

projects.name: Bowser Affordable Rental Homes (22 Units)
projects.blurb: 22 new affordable rental homes have opened in Bowser, BC, offering below-market rents for seniors and other residents.

projects.name: Vancouver Affordable Homes for Families and Seniors (100+ Units)
projects.developer: Province of BC / Government of Canada
projects.blurb: More than 100 new homes for families and seniors have opened in Vancouver, supported by federal and provincial funding.

projects.name: Regent Park Affordable Rental Homes (271 Units)
projects.blurb: The federal government announced over $86 million to build 271 affordable, secure rental homes in Toronto's Regent Park neighbourhood.

projects.name: 11 Brock Ave Mass Timber Affordable Homes
projects.blurb: The first ever public developer-led mass timber affordable housing development at 11 Brock Ave. will create 42 rent-geared-to-income and supportive homes, set for completion in Fall 2026.

projects.name: Township of Langley BC Builds Rental Homes (250 Units)
projects.blurb: Nearly 250 rental homes are underway in the Township of Langley through the BC Builds program, part of B.C.'s $19-billion housing investment.

projects.name: Yellowhead Tribal Council Belvedere Affordable Housing Project
projects.blurb: Two four-storey buildings will provide 149 mixed-income units to support First Nation families in the Belvedere area.

projects.name: British Columbia Affordable Housing Fund (117 Units)
projects.blurb: Canada announced over $20.8 million through the Affordable Housing Fund to build 117 units in British Columbia.

projects.name: 10809 111 Avenue NW Affordable Housing
projects.blurb: A new project in Central McDougall that will construct 248 residential units, including at least 106 affordable homes, supported by the City of Edmonton.

projects.name: Kennedy Green Co-op Homes (612 Units)
projects.blurb: The federal government announced over $289 million to build 612 secure, rental co-op homes in Toronto under the Kennedy Green Co-op project.

projects.name: Toronto Rental Homes Announcement (245 Units)
projects.blurb: The federal government announced over $137 million in funding to help build 245 secure, rental homes in Toronto.

projects.name: Burnaby BC Builds Rental Homes Near Transit
projects.blurb: New rental homes are being built near transit in Burnaby through BC Builds, with rents set at approximately 20% below-market rates for all units.

projects.name: Toronto Metropolitan University Affordable Student Housing Phase 1
projects.developer: Toronto Metropolitan University / Province of Ontario
projects.blurb: An $83 million provincial investment supports the first phase of an affordable student housing development at Toronto Metropolitan University.
